name: Backend killed after first token

injection.description: Kill one engine backend process/pod (SIGKILL, no drain) while it is mid-generation on streams that have already delivered at least one token.

expected_gateway_semantics: Each affected stream receives the standardized mid-stream SSE error event (error.type=upstream_error) followed by stream close — never a retry (Contract 1: post-first-token retry would duplicate sampled output and double-bill).
Tokens already relayed before the failure are settled as partial usage under the stream's request ID (partial output is billable).
expected_client_visible_behavior: Mid-stream SSE error event carrying the request ID, then stream close; content received before the failure is retained; the platform never reconnects or re-generates on the client's behalf.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: increases with status_class=5xx, error_class=upstream_error for the interrupted streams
direction=output increases by the partial token counts of the interrupted streams (settled, not discarded)

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: does not increase for the interrupted streams — no retry after first token, ever

abort_condition: Abort the injection if any interrupted stream is re-sent upstream (duplicate generation observed), or if settled usage for interrupted streams diverges more than 1% from client-observed token counts.
